steps.title: Read the zeros of g
steps.content:
From the graph of $y=g(x)$, the zeros are the $x$-values where the graph meets the $x$-axis:
$$x=-2,\quad x=1,\quad x=3$$

steps.title: Shift the zero inputs
steps.content:
For $g(x+28)$ to equal $0$, the input $x+28$ must be one of the zeros of $g$.
$$x+28=-2,\quad x+28=1,\quad x+28=3$$
$$x=-30,\quad x=-27,\quad x=-25$$

steps.title: Match the possible factor
steps.content: In the factored form, $b$ is one of the zeros of $g(x+28)$ because the factor $(x-b)^2$ becomes $0$ when $x=b$. The only listed value among the possible zeros is $-25$. The answer is D.
